qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSE event injection via unescaped id and type fields in Event.String()
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns unescaped newlines in Event.String() SSE serialization, while the advisory concerns TopicSelectorStore match-cache key collisions from underscore-concatenated topic selector and topic strings.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described TopicSelectorStore cache-key collision. The only final qualifying finding is an independent SSE event serialization injection in Event.String().
evidence: Advisory GHSA-hwr4-mq23-wcv5 describes a cache-key collision where TopicSelectorStore used k = "m_" + topicSelector + "_" + topic, allowing distinct selector/topic pairs containing underscores to share a match-cache entry.
The final session manifest lists only sse-event-injection-unescaped-id-type as a final finding.

That finding reports publisher-controlled id/type newlines reaching Event.String() and being written to subscriber SSE streams without escaping; it does not mention TopicSelectorStore, topic selector cache keys, match-cache poisoning, or selector/topic collision authorization bypass.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was produced.
